_strict_reconciliation.basis: AU Stage B auto-label strict tier was reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine findings; original auto-label breadth is preserved in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. This is internal benchmark cleanup, not legal advice or procurement-grade review.
_strict_reconciliation.strict_source: PreSendReviewEngine.review(..., review_profile='strict') after AU Stage B precision guards

_strict_reconciliation_followups.basis: MY Stage B precision review added a negative-disclaimer guard for 'does not contain material non-public information'. This AU negative fixture used the same disclaimer shape, so the span was removed from strict must_detect and retained as must_not_detect.

_strict_runtime_reconciliation.reason: candidate human-reviewed Stage B strict runtime baseline reconciliation

ideal_must_detect.matched_text: On 2026-02-20, a cross-border transfer of limited payroll support data to Tuatara Data NZ Ltd (Auckland) was authorised under APP 8
optional form fields default to blank
templated ID labels (e.g., Driver Licence Number) are placeholders only
verification uses internal reference tokens and does not require government identifiers
no full card PANs are shared, and account numbers are tokenised

must_detect.matched_text:
Schedule 3 — Data Handling and Transfer Controls (Australia) — WattlePay Pty Ltd as service provider to Paperbark HR Services Pty Ltd (both APP entities)
Scope includes employment onboarding records, staff health declarations (voluntary), merchant credit assessments, and transaction settlement metadata for Australian operations
This schedule does not contain material non-public information for the purposes of the Corporations Act; any ASX references are to generally available announcements only and are included for illustrative policy context
Approval: On 2026-02-20, a cross-border transfer of limited payroll support data to Tuatara Data NZ Ltd (Auckland) was authorised under APP 8 with contract terms requiring substantially similar protections; fields: pseudonymous employee identifier and salted email hash only; no health, credit, or transaction amounts included
Data minimisation is enforced by field-level whitelists and hashing; optional form fields default to blank, and templated ID labels (e.g., Driver Licence Number) are placeholders only
Consent withdrawal for optional analytics is available at any time via the staff and merchant portals; withdrawal is recorded prospectively and does not affect settlement services
Access and correction requests (DSAR under APP 12/13) may be submitted to privacy.office@wattlepay.example; verification uses internal reference tokens and does not require government identifiers
Where no longer needed for any lawful purpose, datasets are de-identified or destroyed per APP 11.2; legal holds under the Corporations Act and ASIC record-keeping rules override deletion
On 2026-03-05, erasure of optional marketing analytics tags for a former contractor was completed following consent withdrawal; primary employment and tax records were not in scope
Retention schedule: employment records retained for seven years post-termination; merchant credit files retained for five years post-onboarding review; health declaration forms retained for two years unless an incident requires longer retention
Purge jobs run monthly in the AU-Sydney region; the last internal audit (2026-04-30) recorded expected deletions and no unintended data loss
Credit assessment data is processed by Brolga Credit Bureau Pty Ltd as an APP entity under a separate agreement; no full card PANs are shared, and account numbers are tokenised
Insider list templates in our onboarding forms are generic artefacts for listed clients and are not populated by WattlePay; no insider lists are process
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

must_not_detect.reason: Role-noun used as a party descriptor, not PII/MNPI
Negated AU privacy-notice disclaimer; not a live MNPI marker.
